methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $246M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
FuriosaAI, a Series C semiconductor chip company founded by June Paik, has raised $246M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in chips and semiconductors, including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX at comparable or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods yield usable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C forward, implied by the $246M total raised proxy, producing $418M. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies yields $1.9B; the peer set ranges from DEEPX at $529M to SambaNova Systems at $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is not applicable absent disclosed revenue. These two methods triangulate to a median of $1.1B, with a low bound of $355M (stage-progression adjusted down) and a high bound of $2.1B (peer-median adjusted toward the upper quartile).

The wide range—$355M to $2.1B—reflects meaningful dispersion among peer valuations and the limited precision of stage-based models for early-stage hardware. Key uncertainties include FuriosaAI's revenue traction relative to peers and its position in the competitive chip-design landscape. The $1.1B median represents a credible central estimate pending further disclosure of financial and product metrics.
